messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] betadisper - distance form centroids
messages.body_plain:
Hello community,

I have a silly question about betadisper() output interpretation. I am
using the function betadisper() after adonis to test for homogeneity of
variances. I have used it a lot in the years but I never get to look at the
boxplots output with the necessary attention.

betadisp_res_type <-
      betadisper(vegdist(otu, method="bray"), metadata$type)

and I got this results (see attached test.png) when I run

boxplot(betadisp_res_type)

In the ordination plot looks like the Root sample should have smaller
distances to centroids not the Soil samples as shown in the boxplot. Is is
because the PCoA is in 2D but in reality there are many layers where the
distances to the centroids might be bigger than what seen in PC1, PC2.
Am I missing something or interpreting it in a wrong way?

Thanks you very much,

---
Gian

*----- Do not print this email unless you really need to. *
*Save paper and protect the environment! -----*

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Spatial autocorrelation in Maxent models
messages.body_plain:
Hi ?kos

Thank you for your response. Do you mean that I should calculate Moran on
the observations' predictions?

Cheers
Diego

On Sat, Jan 16, 2021, 7:24 PM Bede-Fazekas ?kos <bfalevlist at gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Diego,
>
> Yes, you calculated the spatial autocorrelation of the predicted values
> in this way, which is not too informative and intresting.
> Autocorrelation of the residuals is what you need.
> If you subtract the predictions from the observations (using the simple
> raster algebra or raster::calc()), you can calculate Moran's I (or any
> other spatial autocorrelation measure) of the residuals.
>
> HTH,
> ?kos Bede-Fazekas
>
> 2021.01.15. 12:21 keltez?ssel, Diego Guevara ?rta:
> > Hello everyone
> >
> > I am trying to find a way to test for spatial autocorrelation in Maxent
> > models. From my understanding, this should be tested on the residuals of
> > the model. So far I have only been able to test for Moran I test on the
> > prediction resulting raster using the package ELSA. However, I think I am
> > just testing Moran on the prediction values this way. Would that be
> enough
> > or ok? I will appreciate if someone can give me some insight.
> >
> > Best wishes
> > Diego
> >
> >       [[alternative HTML version deleted]]
> >
> > _______________________________________________
> > R-sig-ecology mailing list
> > R-sig-ecology at r-project.org
> > https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-ecology
>
> _______________________________________________
> R-sig-ecology mailing list
> R-sig-ecology at r-project.org
> https://stat.ethz.ch/mailman/listinfo/r-sig-ecology
>

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
messages.body_snippet:
Hi ?kos

Thank you for your response. Do you mean that I should calculate Moran on
the observations' predictions?

Cheers
Diego

On Sat, Jan 16, 2021, 7:24 PM Bede-Fazekas ?kos <bfalevlist at gmail.co

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Spatial autocorrelation in Maxent models
messages.body_plain:
Hi Diego,

No. I mean that you should calculate Moran's I on the residuals (i.e. 
observations minus the predictions to the observation locations).

Have a nice week,
?kos

messages.subject: [R-sig-eco] Online training - Advanced R programming - 8-12 March
messages.subject_clean: Online training - Advanced R programming - 8-12 March
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,
 
we still have a few places available on our course "Advanced R Programming", which will be delivered remotely from the 8th to the 12th of March.
 
 
The emphasis of this course is on building skills for programming, that go beyond basic R usage, such us: regular expressions, dataviz, building packages, build reproducible reports using RMarkdown, work collaboratively & share your code & materials using github and much more...

 
 
If interested, please check it out: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course47/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/course47/ )
 
For the full list of our courses, please see: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/ )
 
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

Follow us on [ Twitter ]( https://twitter.com/Physacourses )




	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
